steps.title: Read the axes
steps.content: The problem states that $x$ represents length and $y$ represents width. In the point $(8,10)$, the first coordinate is the $x$ value and the second is the $y$ value.

steps.title: Interpret the point
steps.content:
So the point $(8,10)$ represents length $8$ meters and width $10$ meters. It also fits the perimeter condition.
$$2(8)+2(10)=36$$

steps.title: Calculator shortcut
steps.content: Graph the perimeter equation and the point. The point $(8,10)$ lies on the graph for a rectangle with perimeter $36$ meters. The answer is C.
